title: ViewModels must access data through Repositories
description:
IF a class is a ViewModel
THEN it should have a reference to a Repository for data access.
Following the MVVM architecture pattern, ViewModels should not directly access data sources (databases, network). Instead, they should delegate data operations to Repository classes which abstract the data layer.

title: Room @Entity classes must have an @PrimaryKey field
description:
IF a class is annotated with @Entity
THEN it must have a field annotated with @PrimaryKey.
Room requires every @Entity to have at least one field marked as @PrimaryKey to uniquely identify rows in the database table. Without it, the Room compiler will produce a build error.

title: ViewModels must not hold Activity or Context references
description:
IF a class extends ViewModel
THEN it must not have fields of type Activity or Context.
ViewModels outlive Activities and Fragments. Holding a reference to an Activity or Context in a ViewModel causes memory leaks because the ViewModel prevents the Activity from being garbage collected after it is destroyed.

title: DAO interfaces must be annotated with @Dao
description:
IF an interface is a Data Access Object (name contains 'Dao')
THEN it must have the @Dao annotation.
Room uses the @Dao annotation to identify interfaces that define database operations. Without @Dao, Room will not generate the implementation for the interface, and database queries will not work.

title: LiveData must not be exposed as MutableLiveData
description:
IF a class extends ViewModel
THEN its MutableLiveData fields must not be public or protected.
A ViewModel owns the data it holds and is the only component permitted to change it. Activities and Fragments observe that data, and exposing the mutable MutableLiveData type to them allows a UI component to overwrite state it should only be reading. Keeping the mutable holder private and publishing it as immutable LiveData preserves a single writer for each value.

title: Use Cases must have a single execute() method
description:
IF a class is a Use Case (name contains 'UseCase')
THEN it must have an execute() method.
In Clean Architecture each Use Case, or Interactor, encapsulates exactly one piece of business logic. Callers reach that logic through a single named entry point, so a Use Case presents one behaviour to the layers above it rather than a collection of loosely related public methods whose calling order the caller must know.

title: ViewModels must not make direct network calls
description:
IF a class extends ViewModel
THEN it must not contain fields of type Retrofit, OkHttpClient, or HttpURLConnection.
ViewModels belong to the presentation layer and hold no networking logic. A network client inside a ViewModel binds UI state to one transport implementation, leaves the ViewModel untestable without a live server, and bypasses the data layer that already abstracts remote and local sources behind a Repository.
